name: Can I get workers comp if my work environment made my asthma or pre-existing condition worse?
description: Yes, you are eligible if work conditions aggravated or accelerated your condition. You must provide medical evidence showing a distinct worsening of your health

mainEntity.name: Can I get workers comp if my work environment made my asthma or pre-existing condition worse?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, you are eligible if work conditions aggravated or accelerated your condition. You must provide medical evidence showing a distinct worsening of your health specifically due to workplace exposure.
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, you can still recover benefits, but it is more complex. In most jurisdictions like Utah, you must prove that your work environment "interally or permanently" aggravated or accelerated the pre-existing condition. The insurance company will try to "apportion" the claim, meaning they only want to pay for the "new" damage. Having a doctor compare your diagnostic imaging (like X-rays or MRIs) from before the exposure to your current state is vital. If work made a manageable condition unmanageable, it is a compensable injury. We focus on the "change in baseline" to ensure you aren't denied just because you weren't 100% healthy before.
